value.found_quote: [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web) runs each session in an isolated, Anthropic-managed virtual machine. A network proxy enforces a default allowlist, and a separate proxy holds your GitHub token outside the sandbox while issuing scoped credentials for repository access inside it.
value.rationale: The page describes Claude Code on the web running agent sessions on Anthropic-managed VMs that clone repositories from GitHub and access them with scoped credentials. However, it specifically references GitHub (the SaaS service) — it says nothing about GHES (GitHub Enterprise Server), self-hosted GitHub instances, cloning GHES repos, or pushing changes back to GHES. The claim's specific GHES support is not mentioned anywhere on the page.
